content.problemStatement: Given two space separated integers as user inputs, aaa and bbb, print the sum of a2a^{\text{2}}a2 and b3b^{\text{3}}b3.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers a and b (1 ≤ a, b ≤ 1000).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the value a² + b³.

solution.approach: For each test case we need to compute a² (a multiplied by itself) and b³ (b multiplied by itself three times) and output their sum. Both operations are O(1). We simply read the inputs, perform the two multiplications using 64‑bit integers to avoid overflow, add the results and print. The algorithm runs in linear time with respect to the number of test cases and uses constant extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());for(int i=0;i<T;i++){StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());long a=Long.parseLong(st.nextToken());long b=Long.parseLong(st.nextToken());long ans=a*a + b*b*b;sb.append(ans);if(i<T-1) sb.append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
